steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence introduces exoplanets "at vast distances from [blank]" and then lists three examples: TRAPPIST-1 b, Kepler-102 f, and over 4,000 others. A colon is needed after "Earth" to introduce this list, and the first list item must begin immediately after the colon.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Earth: at 41 light-years away,: the colon follows the independent clause and correctly introduces the list of exoplanet examples. The first item ("at 41 light-years away, the super Earth TRAPPIST-1 b") flows naturally after the colon. ✓
  • Choice B — Earth at 41 light-years away:: places the colon after "41 light-years away," making it seem like the list starts with TRAPPIST-1 b alone, and detaches "at 41 light-years away" from its exoplanet. ✗
  • Choice C — Earth, at 41 light-years away,: a comma after "Earth" creates an interruptive appositive reading, not a list introduction. No colon introduces the series, so the list structure is lost. ✗
  • Choice D — Earth at 41 light-years away,: omits all punctuation after "Earth," so there is nothing to signal the start of the list. The sentence runs together without a clear boundary. ✗
A is correct.
